response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack and web engineer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and event-driven architecture experience, but his C++ background is limited to a six-month role at National Instruments over a decade ago and a bachelor thesis project — neither constituting the sustained, hands-on C++ expertise this role demands. The position requires 8–12 years of active C++ development, deep STL and multi-threading mastery, industrial protocol knowledge (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), and IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity experience, none of which appear meaningfully in the resume. His domain background in web platforms, direct mail automation, and GIS does not transfer to industrial automation manufacturing.
strengths: Extensive event-driven and distributed systems experience (GISCollective, Sociomantic at millions of RPS) shows awareness of scalable, message-driven architectures
Low-latency, high-performance programming with DLang async I/O and caching at Sociomantic demonstrates performance engineering mindset adjacent to C++ concerns
Active mentoring track record through TDD dojos, workshops, and conference talks aligns with the team mentorship expectation
concerns: C++ is absent from the skills section and surfaces only in a 6-month NI role (2014–2015) and a thesis project — nowhere near the depth required for a senior C++ architect position
No evidence of industrial cybersecurity knowledge (IEC 62443, SDL) or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0, MQTT) anywhere in the resume
Educational background (BS Computer Science + Fine Arts) does not meet the B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech requirement, and there is no industrial automation or embedded systems domain exposure

key_factors.factor: No IEC 62443 or industrial security knowledge
